definition: A behavioral design profile derived from cross-title patterns; it excludes proprietary Zelda expression and cannot override clone-risk vetoes.

dimensions.description: Visible landmarks and anomalies invite voluntary route choice.
Environmental outcomes remain consistent enough for observation and experiment.
Capability, knowledge, or world state reinterprets previously known space.
Landmarks, loops, and player-opened shortcuts build spatial memory.
Safe action and feedback teach before explanatory text.
A core rule is practiced, varied, combined, and examined in the finale.
Rewards create future choices instead of only increasing completion count.
The player can explain the solution as a personally tested inference.
Map state, NPC behavior, and performance cues share one causal fact model.
Failure communicates cause and preserves learning or a safe checkpoint.

cloneRiskVetoes: Same source objects in the same action sequence
Proprietary names, lore, dialogue, visual identity, UI, music, or sound signatures
scoringRule: Score flavor only after canon, runtime, recovery, and clone-risk hard gates pass. A high flavor score cannot cancel a veto.
